# Airport Game
This is a simple, casual flappy bird styled game designed to be played against other passengers at the airport gates.

https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/205794221/575988838-7403b492-dbd3-44d7-88eb-2469d8eb89aa.mov?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTUiLCJleHAiOjE3NzU3NDQzMzMsIm5iZiI6MTc3NTc0NDAzMywicGF0aCI6Ii8yMDU3OTQyMjEvNTc1OTg4ODM4LTc0MDNiNDkyLWRiZDMtNDRkNy04OGViLTI0NjlkOGViODlhYS5tb3Y_.FFRWpL2LFhpsj4QIPd-dVHe2WK_WNIWnRncLSdlv8l4

## How It's Made:

**Tech used:** C#, JavaScript, Java, Unity, Springboot

The server has a very simple controller-service-DB layered structure. As this game is meant to be played with people waiting at the same gate, it has a business logic of retreiving top 10 leaderboard of the gate the player's in. H2 database is used for simple, quick data tracking as well as JPA repository for easy Java based queries and methods. The game(client) is powered by Unity Engine, developed using c# and javascript codes to implement flappy bird styled mechanism and to communicate with backend through APIs. 

## Lessons Learned:

Bridging the gap bewtween the frontend and backend, designing APIs with different data structure and business logic in mind.
